---
applyTo: "**/*.go"
description: "Go coding conventions for the prometheus-collector repository."
---

# Go Conventions

- Group imports in order: stdlib, third-party, internal (`github.com/prometheus-collector/*`). Separate each group with a blank line.
- Use package aliases for internal imports when the package name is ambiguous, e.g., `shared "github.com/prometheus-collector/shared"`.
- Always check and propagate errors with `if err != nil { return err }`. Never silently discard errors.
- Use `log.Println()` and `log.Fatalf()` for logging — do not introduce alternative logging libraries.
- Use `strings.EqualFold()` for case-insensitive string comparisons, not `strings.ToLower()`.
- Use `os.Getenv()` for reading environment variables. Do not hardcode configuration values.
- YAML parsing uses `gopkg.in/yaml.v2` in most modules — do not switch to v3 without explicit reason.
- Build Go binaries with security flags: `-buildmode=pie -ldflags '-linkmode external -extldflags=-Wl,-z,now'`.
- Retain Apache-2.0 SPDX license headers on files derived from the OpenTelemetry Collector.
- Do not modify files marked with `// Code generated by mdatagen. DO NOT EDIT.` — regenerate them instead.
- Use table-driven tests (`[]struct{}` test tables) with `github.com/stretchr/testify/require` for assertions.
